meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to perform idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A professional guide for intermediate founders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop building in a vacuum. The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping micro-SaaS MVPs that solve specific, paid pain points. By using Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can launch a validated, revenue-generating product in days, not months. Real market data beats vanity projects every time.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with projects that were technically perfect but commercially dead. You aren't suffering from a lack of coding skills; you are suffering from a lack of market signals. Staring at an empty GitHub profile is a symptom of a deeper issue: building for yourself instead of building for a customer who is actively looking to solve an expensive problem.

Today, we shift your focus from 'writing code' to 'engineering outcomes.' By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, we will collapse the distance between a raw idea and a validated, deployed application. You don't need a massive portfolio; you need one product that proves you can identify a problem, build a solution, and capture value.

steps.title: Identify High-Value Pain Points
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, use Antigravity to analyze market trends. We define a problem that has existing search volume but poor UX solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current landscape for 'B2B workflow automation' and identify three specific, high-frequency manual tasks that lack a modern, AI-native solution. Provide a market viability score for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research potential SaaS niches with high willingness-to-pay and low current market saturation.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Scope
steps.description: Define the 'Minimum' in MVP. We use Claude Code to strip away non-essential features, ensuring you only build the core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean technical architecture for a SaaS MVP that automates [Targeted Pain Point]. Include only the necessary database schema and API routes for a 48-hour build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a technical specification document for a lean MVP focused on core functionality only.'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate the boilerplate and infrastructure to ensure your app is production-ready from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Supabase integration, Stripe authentication, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the project structure follows industry best practices for modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure using the architectural plan provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Implement the core logic using Claude Code's contextual awareness to ensure the code is clean, tested, and high-performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic for the core automation engine that handles [Specific User Task]. Include error handling, rate limiting, and logging for production observability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core engine logic following the provided documentation and architectural constraints.'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a live environment and set up automated feedback collection to validate your assumptions immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel and include a feedback widget implementation that logs user session data directly to a Supabase table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the production deployment pipeline and integrate the user feedback collection module.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature parity with incumbents.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests before implementation to ensure your logic is bulletproof.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a business case study, not just a storage unit for code.
Implement an automated waitlist or landing page before finishing the backend to measure actual interest.
Always build with observability; if you can't track usage, you can't validate the product.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend the next 48 hours in cold outreach. Contact 50 potential users in your target niche. If you get zero sign-ups, pivot the messaging or the core feature set immediately based on the feedback you captured in the Supabase logs.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repository. Build, deploy, and validate your way to your first paying customer today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
